evals.name: cross-context-search-project
evals.prompt: Search everything we know about Project Nighthawk. Pull up all decisions, meeting notes, delegation records, and people involved across all memory layers.
evals.expected_output: A structured search result showing findings from all four memory layers: project memory (Nighthawk status, milestones), meeting notes/decision log (all decisions related to Nighthawk), people memory (everyone involved), delegation log (any active delegations), and a recommended next step.
expectations: Search covers all four memory layers simultaneously — project, meeting notes, people, terminology, delegation log
Results are grouped by layer so the user can see where each piece of information comes from
RECOMMENDED NEXT STEP section provides a specific actionable recommendation based on the search results — search without action is information without utility
If any layer has no results, it says so explicitly rather than omitting the section

evals.name: search-decision-history
evals.prompt: When did we decide to approve the analytics budget? Find the decision record with the date, who was involved, and any conditions that were attached.
evals.expected_output: A targeted search of the decision log first, cross-referenced with meeting notes, surfacing the specific decision, date, context, who was involved, and any conditions — with recommended next step.
expectations: Search prioritises the decision log for this type of query ('when did we decide')
Cross-references with meeting notes to provide full context around the decision
Surfaces the complete decision including date, participants, and any conditions or caveats
If the decision is not found in workplace memory, says so explicitly — does not fabricate

evals.name: negative-should-use-workplace-context
evals.prompt: Add a new project to my workplace memory. The project is called Phoenix, it is P1 priority, and Omar is the owner. Current milestone is requirements gathering.
evals.expected_output: This query should be handled by workplace-context, not workplace-search. Adding a new project entry to work.local.md is a workplace-context function.
expectations: This query is about adding a new entry to workplace memory — it matches workplace-context triggers (add project, workplace memory)
Workplace-search is for searching and retrieving existing entries, not creating new ones

evals.name: negative-should-use-executive-brief
evals.prompt: Prep me for the Nighthawk discussion with the board. I need the situation summary, key risks, stakeholder positions, and my recommended approach.
evals.expected_output: This query should be handled by executive-brief, not workplace-search. A situation brief with recommended approach is an executive-brief function.
expectations: This query is about a situation brief with recommended approach and stakeholder analysis — it matches executive-brief triggers (prep me for, what do I need to know before, situation brief)
Workplace-search retrieves raw information from memory layers, it does not produce situation briefs with recommended approaches
